channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Nintendo Switch Sports Resort brings another 12 games to Wuhu Island
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Switch Sports Resort has been revealed for, inviting you to Wuhu Island (not in a The Sims kind of way) to play 12 new and returning sports.

Switch Sports Resort is coming out on 22nd October exclusively for Nintendo Switch 2.

Using the Joy-Con 2 for motion controls, Switch Sports Resort will feature the following sports:

  • Boxing
  • Table Tennis
  • Archery
  • Tennis
  • Volleyball
  • Bowling
  • Basketball
  • Golf
  • Thumb Wrestling
  • Skateboarding
  • Power Cruising
  • Prop Plane

And you can also warm up with Jump Rope.

A bunch of these are returning from the last Switch Sports. Of the new ones, there’s some inventive new controller uses, such as needing mouse mode to play Skateboarding, or using Joy-Con as handlebars for the jet ski racing of Power Cruising.

You’ll be able to play once again as your Mii or a SportsMii, which has enhanced customisation options.

Online play will be included, with details on the player count for each game to be revealed at a later date.

Back with the last game, Jason said in our Switch Sports review, “Nintendo Switch Sports is a fun reminder of the Wii Sports craze, bringing back some classic sports and adding some new ones that make use of the Joy-Con and motion controls in more advanced ways. It’s all just a bit shallow, though, and difficult to wholeheartedly recommend unless you expect to play online a lot or constantly break it out for local head-to-head battles.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/nintendo-switch-sports-resort-brings-another-12-games-to-wuhu-island/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Orbitals brings its anime It Takes Two co-op action to Switch 2 in September
item.content:encoded:
Orbitals has a release date, the 90s anime riff on the beloved It Takes Two style co-op adventure set to launch exclusively for Switch 2 on 3rd September 2026.

The graphical style really shines through here, really capturing the look and feel of classic Japanese animation to go alongside the familiar looking split-screen co-op. The game sees explorers Maki and Omura setting out from their home to try and save it from an impending cosmic storm. They’ll be making use of separate abilities to support each others’ adventuring, using things like a grapple gun to pull out platforms, or cool lava with a water gun.

The game can also be played across multiple systems using GameShare, so you don’t have to play on TV or with a single Joy-Con.

Announced back at The Game Awards and then reappearing in a Partner Direct earlier this year, the game is in development by Tokyo-based indie team Shapefarm, but with assistance from animation house Studio Massket to craft its cutscenes. The game will have both Japanese and English audio dubs.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/orbitals-brings-its-anime-it-takes-two-co-op-action-to-switch-2-in-september/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: All the ports, announcements and release dates from the Nintendo Direct (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
The Nintendo Direct has just wrapped, and there’s a flood of game announcements coming to Nintendo Switch 2 (and some to Switch as well). There’s so many, in fact, that Nintendo broke a lot of them down into montages for the ports.

DMC5? Space Marine 2? Lies of P? Rise of the Tomb Raider? Yes. They, and a whole lot more are coming to Switch 2 this year or next.

We’ll get to the big list of release dates, but first, let’s look at the big juicy announcements!

  • The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time remake has been revealed for Switch 2
  • Final Fantasy Resonance brings an all new HD-2D entry in the series to the Switch and Switch 2
  • Xenoblade Genesis is a brand new game in the Xenoblade series, arriving next year
  • Fire Emblem: Fortune’s Weave launches in September for Switch 2
  • Xenoblade Chronicles 1, 2 and 3 are all getting Switch 2 upgrades this year, starting today!
  • Pokémon Pokopia goes under the sea with free update and DLC Expansion Pass this August
  • Kingdom Hearts 4 is also coming to Switch 2, and there’s a new (ish) trailer
  • Splatoon Raiders gets a new trailer packed with features
  • Check out Rhythm Paradise Groove’s timing and multiplayer modes

OK, and now? Here’s the barrage of release dates:

  • SnowRunner – Out Today
  • Rise of the Tomb Raider: 20 Year Celebration – Out Today
  • Observer: System Redux – 18th June
  • Devil May Cry 5: Devil Hunter Edition – 23rd June 2026
  • Fitness Boxing 3 Switch 2 Edition – 16th July
  • Lies of P Complete Edition – 6th August 2026 (digital), 2nd October 2026 (physical)
  • Metal Gear Solid Master Collection 2 – 27th August 2026
  • Final Fantasy XIV – Early Access in August 2026
  • RuneScape: Dragonwilds – 15th September 2026
  • Dragon’s Dogma 2 – 9th October 2026
  • Tales of Eternia Remastered – 16th October 2026
  • Hello Kitty Partyland – 29th October 2026
  • Metaphor: ReFantazio – 12th November 2026
  • Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2 – Holiday 2026
  • Day Z – TBA 2026
  • Stellar Blade – TBC 2026
  • Atelier Karia: The Night Kingdom & the Guide of Memories – Early 2027
  • Everbloom – Spring 2027

Here’s also a bunch of the announcements and trailers that we haven’t broken out into separate posts.

Orbitals is coming out on 3rd September, exclusively for Switch 2

Big Walk is taking a stroll on 4th August

One Piece: Grand Gourmet announced

Ninjala 2: The Uncharted Planet revealed

The DK Challenge arrives, and there’s a Super Mario collab in DK Bananza

Jujutsu Kaisen Rumble is a Vampire Survivors Survation

Lords of the Fallen II is coming to Switch 2

Muramasa: Revenant Blades announced

Nintendo Switch Sports Resort

Star Fox has a demo out today

Pikuniku 2 revealed

Dragon Quest Monsters: The Withered World

The full Kingdom Hearts Saga to date is coming to Switch 2

And that, just about, is the whole lot! A packed Nintendo Direct with plenty to look forward to through the rest of 2026!



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/09/all-the-ports-announcements-and-release-dates-from-the-nintendo-direct-june-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
